oracle_spec.assertion: The only precondition the code checks before the assignment is `sess->state != PSMGR_STATE_INIT` (blocks re-entry) and psmgr_validate_role(opt.role) (gates which role values are accepted); neither checks that enc_class/xfrm_type have been negotiated. When opt.role == PSMGR_ROLE_FORWARD the code sets sess->state directly to PSMGR_STATE_NEGO_2, the same signal value that on the PRIMARY path is only reached after psmgr_validate_enc_class/psmgr_validate_xfrm_type succeed in the NEGO_1 case -- so an attacker can set the 'reached NEGO_2' signal field while enc_class/xfrm_type were never validated or initialized, violating the invariant any later code relies on (state==NEGO_2 implies validated transform params). Confirmed real: no check in this snippet ties the state jump to enc/xfrm validation.

assume_c: xfrm_type == XFRM_SCATTER || xfrm_type == XFRM_WINDOW || xfrm_type == XFRM_REFLECT || xfrm_type == XFRM_COMMIT

(xfrm_type == XFRM_SCATTER && ((enc_mode == MODE_LINEAR && proc_param <= MAX_SCATTER_LINEAR) || (enc_mode == MODE_INTERLEAVE && proc_param <= MAX_SCATTER_INTERLEAVE) || (enc_mode != MODE_LINEAR && enc_mode != MODE_INTERLEAVE && proc_param <= MAX_SCATTER_OTHER))) || ((xfrm_type == XFRM_WINDOW || xfrm_type == XFRM_REFLECT) && ((enc_mode == MODE_LINEAR && proc_param <= MAX_WINREF_LINEAR) || (enc_mode == MODE_INTERLEAVE && proc_param <= MAX_WINREF_INTERLEAVE) || (enc_mode != MODE_LINEAR && enc_mode != MODE_INTERLEAVE && proc_param <= MAX_WINREF_OTHER))) || (xfrm_type == XFRM_COMMIT && ((enc_mode == MODE_LINEAR && proc_param <= MAX_COMMIT_LINEAR) || (enc_mode == MODE_INTERLEAVE && proc_param <= MAX_COMMIT_INTERLEAVE) || (enc_mode != MODE_LINEAR && enc_mode != MODE_INTERLEAVE && proc_param <= MAX_COMMIT_OTHER)))
oracle_spec.assertion: opt.proc_param is gated by a per-(xfrm_type,enc_mode) param_max (max literal 127) via 'if (opt.proc_param > param_max) break;' with an unreachable-default fallback for invalid xfrm_type; the gate is exhaustive over all reachable branches so proc_param can never exceed 127 before being stored, so this is a false positive.

assume_c: xfrm_type == XFRM_SCATTER || xfrm_type == XFRM_WINDOW || xfrm_type == XFRM_REFLECT || xfrm_type == XFRM_COMMIT

(xfrm_type == XFRM_SCATTER && ((enc_mode == MODE_LINEAR && proc_param <= MAX_SCATTER_LINEAR) || (enc_mode == MODE_INTERLEAVE && proc_param <= MAX_SCATTER_INTERLEAVE) || (enc_mode != MODE_LINEAR && enc_mode != MODE_INTERLEAVE && proc_param <= MAX_SCATTER_OTHER))) || ((xfrm_type == XFRM_WINDOW || xfrm_type == XFRM_REFLECT) && ((enc_mode == MODE_LINEAR && proc_param <= MAX_WINREF_LINEAR) || (enc_mode == MODE_INTERLEAVE && proc_param <= MAX_WINREF_INTERLEAVE) || (enc_mode != MODE_LINEAR && enc_mode != MODE_INTERLEAVE && proc_param <= MAX_WINREF_OTHER))) || (xfrm_type == XFRM_COMMIT && ((enc_mode == MODE_LINEAR && proc_param <= MAX_COMMIT_LINEAR) || (enc_mode == MODE_INTERLEAVE && proc_param <= MAX_COMMIT_INTERLEAVE) || (enc_mode != MODE_LINEAR && enc_mode != MODE_INTERLEAVE && proc_param <= MAX_COMMIT_OTHER)))
oracle_spec.assertion: opt.proc_param is gated by a per-(xfrm_type,enc_mode) param_max (max literal 127) via 'if (opt.proc_param > param_max) break;' with an unreachable-default fallback for invalid xfrm_type; the gate is exhaustive over all reachable branches so proc_param can never exceed 127 before being stored, so this is a false positive.

oracle_spec.assertion: psmgr_desc.token stays within the domain its consumer's agreement governs
assumptions: producer sets token without enforcing the consumer's cross-field invariant

oracle_spec.assertion: psmgr_desc.token is interpreted consistently across all live consumers
assumptions: resource stays live while read under >= 2 incompatible models

oracle_spec.assertion: pb stays within the object's declared buffer
assumptions: external content written at a variable offset into the object

oracle_spec.assertion: desc governs a bounded, trusted referenced operand
assumptions: access extent/source/dest loaded from the field
